_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the types of law in Australia — criminal and civil — and the place of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander customary law.

_source.w: We are learning about the types of law in Australia — criminal and civil — and the place of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander customary law.
y: Understanding the difference between criminal and civil law helps us know our rights in different situations.
Recognising Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander customary law acknowledges legal systems that existed for thousands of years before colonisation.
This knowledge helps us engage with debates about legal pluralism and justice for all Australians.
eg: Comparing criminal law (offences against the community) with civil law (disputes between individuals).
Investigating examples of criminal cases versus civil cases and how they are handled differently.
Learning about Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander customary law and how it governs community life.
Discussing how customary law relates to the Australian legal system — where it is recognised and where it is not.
Examining a case where customary law principles were considered by an Australian court.
Debating whether Australia's legal system should more formally recognise Indigenous customary law.
